sources.title: Triple-Hormone-Receptor Agonist Retatrutide for Obesity — A Phase 2 Trial
ClinicalTrials.gov record for retatrutide obesity phase 2 study

claims.statement: Retatrutide is also referred to as LY3437943 and is described in clinical-trial literature as a triple hormone receptor agonist targeting GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptor pathways.
reviewFlags: investigational identity should be reviewed against latest sponsor/regulatory records

claims.statement: A phase 2 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial studied subcutaneous retatrutide in adults with obesity or overweight plus at least one weight-related condition, with body-weight change as a primary endpoint.

context.population: adults with obesity or overweight with at least one weight-related condition

extractedEvidence.quote: We conducted a phase 2, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial involving adults who had a body-mass index...

reviewFlags: do not generalize phase 2 evidence to approved-use claims

claims.statement: BioStack should treat retatrutide as investigational in this pilot unless a jurisdiction-specific regulatory approval source is added; clinical-trial evidence should not be presented as an approved-product label.
reviewFlags: review latest FDA/DailyMed/EMA status before publication
vendor or compounded claims require misinformation monitoring

reviewReasons: Pilot emerging evidence packet requires human review before publication.
